metadata.label: Physical description
metadata.value: A machine readable hierarchical Digital Cultural Heritage Catalogue compatible with IIIF 2.0 Presentation API.

metadata.label: Notes
metadata.value:
IIIFDexir is a large-scale, nested IIIF Collection ecosystem built on the International Image Interoperability Framework (IIIF) Presentation API. It features a root collection with deeply hierarchical sub-collections, integrating IIIF resources (Manifests and Collections) from approximately 70 international GLAM institutions.
Key technical aspects:

metadata.label: Structure
metadata.value: Tree-like nesting of Collections and Manifests in JSON-LD format, enabling recursive browsing in compliant viewers.

metadata.label: Semantic Enrichment
metadata.value: Augmented with custom RDF ontology (Turtle format) defining classes (e.g., ResourceCollection, DigitalResource, Creator) and properties (e.g., hasCreator, hasTGNPlace), aligned with standards like Schema.org, Wikidata, AAT, and TGN.

metadata.label: Folio and Content Decomposition
metadata.value: A core innovation in IIIFDexir is the granular decomposition of manuscript folios leveraging the IIIF Canvas concept (IIIF Presentation API). Each folio is modeled as a Canvas, which serves as a spatial container for its visual and textual content. This is further broken down into multiple content elements—discrete, semantically rich units that enable precise annotation, querying, and interoperability

metadata.label: Focus
metadata.value: Thematic aggregation of Persian/Iranian cultural materials, including "Departed Collections" that logically reunify physically dispersed assets.

metadata.label: Queryable
metadata.value: The data is fully RDF compliant, enabling advanced queries using SPARQL across collections based on semantic annotations.

metadata.label: Data Visualization
metadata.value: The observation of the whole collection structure and relationships is facilitated through any graph visualization can understand RDF.

metadata.label: Source
metadata.value: Hosted at https://github.com/MehranDHN/IIIFCollection (root collection typically at paths like IIIFCollection/IIIF2Collection.json).

en: Detached folio from the Manuscript; Khamsa (Quintet) by Nizami; text: Persian in black nasta'liq script; illuminated headings in white; 239 folios with 4 sarlawhs (fols. 1 verso, 74 verso, 124 verso, 172 verso), 17 paintings (fols. 12 recto, 20 verso, 36 verso, 39 verso, 61 verso, 100recto, 102 verso, 105 verso, 107 verso, 113recto, 117 verso, 133 verso, 149 verso, 152recto, 166 verso, 212recto), and 60 imprints of three seals; standard page: 4 columns, 21 lines of text. Binding: The manuscript is in a nineteenth-century lacquer-painted binding and doublures of gold floral motifs on red ground.
